title: Melampodium minimum
taxonomy: Melampodium minimum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, which also include composites and asters. The classification of Melampodium minimum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Melampodium minimum, also known as blackfoot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Melampodium minimum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as melampolides and dehydrocoumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried root to treat various health conditions, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
